acceptance_rules.agent_state_similarity: mean across users >= 0.8 (no baseline significance required)
acceptance_rules.agent_state_similarity_detail: mean of per-user means across strategies; threshold on global mean; not a baseline contrast
acceptance_rules.behavioral_similarity_strategy: paired Wilcoxon one-sided greater vs baseline C when 3-class classifier gate passes; otherwise soft-only
acceptance_rules.classifier_gate: formal acceptance requires independent train/gate labels, class minima, separation, non-TFIDF embedding engine, and 3-class macro-F1 gate pass
acceptance_rules.formal_baseline_c: skip_population_average_implant is test-only and blocks formal acceptance when present
acceptance_rules.per_strategy_comparisons: same Wilcoxon rules computed separately per split strategy (see per_strategy_comparisons)
acceptance_rules.pilot: run_pilot_validation estimates sd of per-user semantic (personality vs baseline A) and behavioral (personality vs baseline C) mean differences; suggested_min_users uses max of thresholds when either exceeds pilot_sd_threshold
acceptance_rules.semantic_embedding_gate: formal acceptance requires sentence_embedding_cosine; TF-IDF fallback is development-only
acceptance_rules.semantic_similarity: paired Wilcoxon one-sided greater vs baseline A (p < 0.05) and mean paired diff > 0; one paired sample per user (mean across split strategies)
acceptance_rules.statistical_engine: scipy.stats.wilcoxon(alternative='greater'); no fallback p-values are valid for formal acceptance
acceptance_rules.topic_gate: topic_split_not_applicable users are excluded from topic statistics, but valid topic users must still meet required_users

debug_readiness_gate.policy: debug-only gate for state/profile differentiation before mini-formal
